iran_nuke.notes: Day 15 02:04 — FALLING STABLE. No new nuclear signals. Kharg Island strikes are conventional oil infrastructure targeting, not nuclear. Parchin remains conventional. Netanyahu press conference focused on regime change rhetoric, no nuclear option discussed. Latent capability (460kg 60% uranium) remains but operational pathway degraded. Nuclear track continues de-escalation. 10 stable.

iran_conventional.notes: Day 15 02:04 — IMMINENT RISING. MAJOR: (1) US destroys 'every military target' on Kharg Island — Trump says 'totally obliterated.' Oil refinery employees report ~2 hours of nonstop explosions/earthquake-like shaking. (2) Trump threatens Kharg OIL infrastructure next: 'If they keep blocking ships... watch what happens.' First explicit threat to destroy Iran's oil export capacity. (3) US KC-135 refueling plane crashes in Iraq — additional US military losses. (4) Netanyahu first press conference: threatens Khamenei + Hezbollah, acknowledges regime toppled 'from within.' (5) IEA announces record 400M barrel strategic reserve release to counter Hormuz disruption. (6) Canada contributes 23.6M barrels. (7) Dubai: 21 charged under cybercrime laws for posting missile strike videos — UAE information control. (8) Iran casualties: 1,444 killed / 18,551 injured (Health Ministry). Israeli strikes continue on Tehran overnight. 83→86.

israel_lebanon.notes: Day 15 02:04 — IMMINENT STABLE. No new Lebanon-specific developments. Netanyahu threatened Hezbollah in press conference but no operational changes. Zrarieh Bridge + Gaza-scale threats + Beirut leaflets remain latest. 773 dead in Lebanon. Ground incursion timing still TBD as Israel focused on Iran. 82 stable.

turkey.notes: CRITICAL — RISING STABLE. Third intercept confirmed by Politico/NYT/Reuters/Al Jazeera. Western officials: March 4 missile specifically targeted Incirlik (houses US B61 nuclear weapons). NATO spokeswoman Allison Hart confirmed 'ballistic missile heading to Turkey' intercepted. Turkey demands Tehran clarify. Article 4 pressure mounting. No 4th intercept yet — key threshold watch. 36 stable.

india.notes: Stable deterrent. No new signals. IndiGo introduces fuel surcharge due to Iran crisis — economic impact only. No escalation indicators.

russia.notes: Day 15 02:04 — RISING. NEW: (1) US formally issues sanctions license — authorizes delivery/sale of Russian crude loaded before Mar 12, valid through Apr 11. Concrete policy, not just rhetoric. (2) European allies 'riled' per Reuters — Macron calls out Russia hypocrisy on ceasefire demands. (3) Merz (Germany) demands Trump account for lifting sanctions. (4) Putin-Trump call covered both Iran + Ukraine. Sanctions relief now operational reality funding Russian war machine. 12→13.

china.notes: Day 15 02:04 — RISING. NEW: (1) ISW/AEI: PRC increased military budget, escalated Taiwan rhetoric, emphasized tech development in 2026. (2) China 'rebuilding grip on North Korea' per Reuters — Kim engagement increasing. (3) China continuing opposition to Iran war. (4) PLA flights remain low but budget ramp signals long-term posture shift. Pacific force gaps widening as US assets shift to ME. 13→14.

north_korea.notes: Stable elevated. No new military provocations. China-DPRK engagement increasing — 'rebuilding grip on North Korea' per Reuters. Iran-DPRK cooperation revival 'highly likely.' OFAC sanctions on DPRK IT networks still active. 16 stable.

russia_ukraine.notes: Day 15 02:04 — RISING. (1) US sanctions license now issued — Russian crude deliveries authorized through Apr 11. Concrete sanctions relief. (2) European allies furious: Macron, Merz demanding accountability. (3) Zelensky: US sought postponement of trilateral talks. (4) Trump deal stalling further as Iran war consumes attention. (5) Russia casualties ~1.28M. (6) Putin-Trump call: no Ukraine breakthrough. 29→30.

pakistan_afghanistan.notes: No new reports in this cycle. Saudi-Pakistan Strategic Mutual Defence Agreement active. China mediating. Stable elevated.

Trump issues formal sanctions license for Russian crude — valid through April 11, European allies furious (Reuters)
Netanyahu first press conference since war — threatens Khamenei + Hezbollah, says regime toppled 'from within' (NYT/CNN)
IEA announces record 400M barrel strategic reserve release to counter Hormuz oil price spike — Canada contributes 23.6M barrels (The Hindu/Reuters)
US KC-135 refueling plane crashes in Iraq — additional US military losses (CBS/NYT)

new: iran_conventional: Kharg Island military targets destroyed — US 'totally obliterated' all military targets on Iran's main oil export hub per Trump, 2 hours of nonstop explosions
iran_conventional: Trump threatens Kharg oil infrastructure — first explicit threat to destroy Iran's oil export capacity if Hormuz remains blocked
russia: US sanctions license issued — concrete policy authorizing Russian crude deliveries through April 11, not just rhetoric
changed_signals.de_escalation_watch: ESCALATION DEEPENS: Day 15 — Kharg Island DESTROYED. US military targets on Iran's main oil hub 'totally obliterated' per Trump. Trump now threatens oil infrastructure itself — would eliminate Iran's primary revenue source. This is the sharpest escalation yet: from military targets to economic infrastructure. KC-135 crash adds to US military losses. Netanyahu acknowledges regime change may require internal forces. IEA releases record 400M barrels — global energy crisis management engaged. European allies furious over Russia sanctions relief. NUCLEAR TRACK: Still de-escalating — Kharg strikes are conventional oil infrastructure targeting. No enrichment escalation. KEY WATCH FOR NEXT 6H: Whether Trump follows through on Kharg oil infrastructure threat (would be irreversible escalation); 4th Turkey intercept → Article 4 trigger; Iran retaliation for Kharg destruction; any sign of Hormuz reopening; European formal response to Russia sanctions relief.

latest_news.headline: US destroys 'every military target' on Kharg Island — Trump threatens oil infrastructure next
latest_news.text: Trump announced US 'totally obliterated' all military targets on Kharg Island, Iran's main oil export hub. Oil refinery employees reported nearly 2 hours of nonstop explosions and airstrikes that 'shook the island like an earthquake.' Trump threatened: if Iran keeps blocking Hormuz, oil infrastructure is next. Senior Iranian Oil Ministry official called attacks 'enormous and destructive.'

latest_news.headline: Trump issues formal sanctions license for Russian crude — valid through April 11, European allies furious
latest_news.text: US Treasury issued license authorizing delivery and sale of Russian crude oil and petroleum products loaded on vessels on or before March 12, valid through midnight April 11. European allies 'riled' — Macron calls out Russia ceasefire hypocrisy. German Chancellor Merz demands Trump account for lifting sanctions. Move directly funds Russian war machine.

latest_news.headline: Netanyahu first press conference since war — threatens Khamenei + Hezbollah, regime change 'from within'
latest_news.text: Netanyahu took questions from reporters for first time since war began. Threatened Iran's new supreme leader and Hezbollah. Acknowledged Israeli attacks may not overthrow regime: 'We are creating the optimal conditions for the overthrow of the regime, but I can't say for certain that the Iranian people would topple it. A regime is toppled from within.'

latest_news.headline: IEA announces record 400M barrel strategic reserve release — Canada contributes 23.6M barrels
latest_news.text: IEA agreed to release record 400 million barrels from strategic stockpiles held by member nations to combat price spike from Hormuz disruption. Canada contributing 23.6M barrels from domestic producers. Largest coordinated oil release in history.

latest_news.headline: US KC-135 refueling plane crashes in Iraq
latest_news.text: US military KC-135 refueling aircraft crashed in Iraq. Details on crew status pending. Adds to US military losses in theater alongside earlier casualties from Iranian strikes.

latest_news.headline: Third Iranian missile intercept over Turkey confirmed — NATO spokeswoman, targeted Incirlik base
latest_news.source: Politico EU / NYT / Reuters / Al Jazeera
latest_news.text: NATO defense systems in eastern Mediterranean shot down third Iranian ballistic missile heading toward Turkish airspace. NATO spokeswoman Allison Hart confirmed interception. Western officials: March 4 missile specifically targeted Incirlik Air Base which houses US B61 nuclear weapons. Turkey demands Tehran clarify. Third incident in ~10 days.

latest_news.headline: Dubai: 21 charged under cybercrime laws for posting missile strike videos
latest_news.text: UAE charged 21 people under cybercrime laws for sharing videos and social media posts about recent missile strikes. Images of fragments striking residential buildings, luxury hotel and Dubai airport circulated widely. UAE cracking down on war information sharing.

latest_news.headline: China increases military budget, escalates Taiwan rhetoric per ISW/AEI — Pacific force gaps widen
latest_news.text: PRC increased military budget, escalated rhetoric towards Taiwan, emphasized emerging tech development in 2026. China 'rebuilding grip on North Korea' per Reuters. US military commanders worried about shrinking stockpiles as Marines redeploy from Okinawa to ME. Pacific force gaps widening. $14B Taiwan arms deal awaiting Trump approval.

outlook: CRITICAL — Global war probability RISING to 46%. Day 15 02:04 — KHARG ISLAND DESTROYED: Sharpest escalation yet. US 'totally obliterated' all military targets on Iran's main oil export hub. Trump threatens oil infrastructure itself next — would eliminate Iran's primary revenue source. This is the line between military strikes and economic warfare. KC-135 crash adds to US military losses. Netanyahu press conference: regime change requires internal forces, threats to Khamenei + Hezbollah. IEA releases record 400M barrels — global energy crisis management engaged. EUROPEAN FURY: Formal sanctions license issued for Russian crude through Apr 11. Macron, Merz demanding accountability. Russia directly benefits from Hormuz crisis. NUCLEAR TRACK: Still de-escalating — Kharg is conventional oil targeting. No enrichment escalation. KEY WATCH FOR NEXT 6H: (1) Does Trump follow through on Kharg oil infrastructure threat? Would be irreversible escalation. (2) 4th Turkey intercept → Article 4 trigger. (3) Iran retaliation for Kharg destruction. (4) Hormuz status — any sign of reopening. (5) European formal response to Russia sanctions license. (6) Iran 'additional fronts' — what and where.
